name: 01-technical-pitch
description: Technical pitch state — speaker making concrete numerical claims about a vector DB (100M embeddings, 0.8ms p99, 40% cost vs Pinecone, HNSW+PQ). Tests claim_made classification and the [fact, talking, question] mix engaging with specific numbers.

transcript.chunk: We're building a vector DB that handles 100 million embeddings with 0.8 millisecond p99 latency.
Compared to Pinecone, our pricing comes in at about 40% cost reduction at this scale.
We use HNSW indexing with product quantization — that's how we hit those latency numbers.

classifier.state_evidence: Speaker is asserting concrete performance and pricing claims (100M embeddings, 0.8ms p99, 40% cost reduction vs Pinecone, HNSW+PQ).

cards.preview: Verified: HNSW with product quantization routinely achieves sub-millisecond p99 at 100M-vector scale.
cards.full_context: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) graphs with PQ compression are the standard high-recall ANN approach used by FAISS and Milvus benchmarks at 100M-plus scale. 0.8ms p99 is plausible on properly-provisioned hardware.
grounded_in: [00:00] We're building a vector DB that handles 100 million embeddings with 0.8 millisecond p99 latency.

tuple.core_claim: sub-ms p99 at 100M scale is industry-standard

cards.preview: The 40% cost framing depends heavily on Pinecone's tier — serverless and pod pricing differ ~3x.
cards.full_context: Pinecone's serverless pricing is dramatically different from their pod-based pricing for the same workload. A 40% reduction vs serverless is a much weaker claim than 40% reduction vs pod pricing.
grounded_in: [00:30] Compared to Pinecone, our pricing comes in at about 40% cost reduction at this scale.

tuple.core_claim: serverless vs pod pricing differ approximately 3x

cards.preview: What's the index build time at 100 million vectors? That's where ops issues typically surface.
cards.full_context: Latency at query time is one dimension; build/rebuild time is another. At 100M scale, HNSW builds can take hours and become an operational bottleneck during reindexing or recovery.
grounded_in: [00:00] We're building a vector DB that handles 100 million embeddings with 0.8 millisecond p99 latency.
[01:00] We use HNSW indexing with product quantization — that's how we hit those latency numbers.

tuple.core_claim: what is index build time at 100 million scale
